rulings.Case Title: CAROL SUI MAN LAM et al VS. MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 6, 2010, line 13, DEFENDANT AMERICAN SECURITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. For reasons stated in ruling on Defendant Mid-Century's motion for summary judgment, triable issues of fact raised whether Mid-Century's rescission valid. ASIC does not explain why the issue of whether Mid-Centrury improperly rescinded its policy is relevant to plaintiff's breach of contract claim. Even if Mid-Century improperly rescinded, ASIC has not shown that this would have affect Wells Fargo's actions and requirements with respect to retaining ASIC's insurance for plaintiff's property. The undisputed facts do not show that ASIC's policy was cancelled or otherwise removed based on Mid-Century's purportedly improper rescission. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL SUI MAN LAM et al VS. MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 6, 2010, line 13, DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof regarding rescission, and triable issues of fact raised. Memorandum of Fire Insurance is initialed on all pages by both plaintiff and the agent. The term "unrepaired damage" is not explained or defined. Lam described the property as a "fixer upper" which should have prompted further inquiry and investigation. Lam's deposition reflects some lack of knowledge about damage to the property and what should be fixed. (Plaintiffs' UMP #53, 71.) Triable issue of fact regarding the agent's duty to inspect the entire property, (Plaintiffs' UMF 103, Neal deposition) and the agent's checklist (#105, 106). Photos of the property show damage the agent should have reported. (Plaintiffs' UMF 171, Neal deposition.) Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant's investigation was proper and led to a "genuine dispute." =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY DUBAN VS. DANIEL BERKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling The San Francisco Superior Court To Pay, From Funds Held By The Court, Monies Necessary To Pay The Judgment Entered In Fthis Action Agaisnt Larry Duban And In Favor Of Judgment Creditor Daniel Berko And The Two Sanctions Awards Made In This Case; Declaration; Requesst For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 6, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S Motion For An Order Compelling The San Francisco Superior Court To Pay, From Funds Held By The Court, Monies Necessary To Pay The Judgment Entered In this Action Agaisnt Larry Duban And In Favor Of Judgment Creditor Daniel Berko And The Two Sanctions Awards Made In This Case. MOTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DUBAN FAILED TO POST A BOND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 917.1 BY DECEMBER 31, 2009. COURT SHALL RELEASE INTERPLEADED FUNDS FROM CASE NUMBER 08-476380 TO DEFENDANT BERKO IN AN AMOUNT EQUAL TO THE JUDGMENT, PLUS COSTS, PLUS THE TWO SANCTIONS ORDERS, PLUS ACCRUED INTEREST. RELEASE OF FUNDS APPROPRIATE BECAUSE THE INTERPLEADER ACTION INVOLVES DUBAN, BERKO, AND OMAN; THE FUNDS IN INTERPLEADER WERE THE RESULT OF A $300,000 SETTLEMENT IN THE WRONGFUL EVICTION CASE WHERE BERKO AND OMAN REPRESENTED DUBAN; BERKO PREVAILED IN THIS MALPRACTICE/FEES CASE AND OBTAINED A $150,000 JUDGMENT AND SANCTIONS ORDERS; DUBAN APPEALED THAT DECISION; AND OMAN DOES NOT OPPOSE THE RELEASE OF FUNDS TO BERKO. =(302/CWW)
